300.prompt: Start with this detail: Duty-free refers to goods sold without certain local taxes in transit zones. Which idea gives the best comparison?
wrongAnswers: Travel as status
Tourism types
Airport life as limbo
300.visibleConnection: The question starts from a concrete detail in Duty-free culture: Duty-free refers to goods sold without certain local taxes in transit zones. It is really asking which answer handles a similar pressure. From inns to hotels is the best connection because it turns rest for travelers into an institution, showing how temporary shelter becomes part of mobility systems. Both ideas ask alpacas to notice that Duty-free shopping makes borders feel pleasant, but it depends on tax rules, security systems, and controlled movement.
